forbiddenClaims: AI summary accuracy percentage, confidence score, or reliability ranking
source-authority ranking or 'most reliable source' / 'best source' verdict over publishers
AI summary presented as the publisher's verdict, Newswire's reporting, an editorial conclusion, or a source of record
context panel presented as editorial analysis, a price forecast, or a buy/sell recommendation
analyst quotes, price targets, ratings, subscriber counts, or read counts not supplied in the brief
the four layers merged into a single Newswire-authored narrative
affiliation with any real news publisher, exchange, index provider, rating agency, or data vendor

rubricAnchors.score1: Sourced publisher text, AI summary, context, and other publishers blur into one Newswire-authored narrative, or the AI summary is presented as the publisher's verdict.
rubricAnchors.score3: The four layers are distinguishable but one boundary is soft — for example context reads as editorial analysis, or a summary point lacks a source link.
rubricAnchors.score5: The four layers are visually and semantically separated at all scroll positions, every AI-summary point traces to its source, context is labelled machine-assembled, and no authoritative verdict is claimed.

rubricAnchors.score1: story-sourced-article, ai-summary-block, context-panel, and other-publishers-list share no common direction system and disagree on how layers are labelled.
rubricAnchors.score3: Shared tokens across the four screens but the machine-generated label or the source-trace treatment is applied inconsistently between screens.
rubricAnchors.score5: A single direction system unifies all four screens, with a consistent layer-labelling system (sourced / AI summary / context / other publishers) and a consistent source-trace treatment.

rubricAnchors.score1: No actionable blueprint; engineers cannot infer the sourced-article, ai-summary-block, context-panel, or other-publishers-list states.
rubricAnchors.score3: Partial blueprints with unresolved no-source-for-point, paywall-detected, single-publisher-only, or context-empty states.
rubricAnchors.score5: Engineers can build all four screens directly, including no-source-for-point, sentence-source-trace-active, paywall-detected, single-publisher-only, and context-empty states with their mobile rules.

rubricAnchors.score1: A direct clone of an existing reader that merges AI synthesis and publisher text or ranks sources by authority.
rubricAnchors.score3: Derivative of common reader patterns but the four-layer separation and source-trace treatment is visually distinguishable.
rubricAnchors.score5: A distinct treatment that makes the boundary between reported fact and machine synthesis unmistakable on a small screen, without ranking sources or claiming accuracy.

rubricAnchors.score1: No accessibility handling and no article-unavailable, no-source-for-point, or single-publisher-only states.
rubricAnchors.score3: Baseline contrast and focus with partial handling of paywall-detected or no-source-for-point.
rubricAnchors.score5: WCAG-aligned contrast and focus across all four screens, with article-unavailable, paywall-detected, no-source-for-point, single-publisher-only, and context-empty states that never substitute the AI summary for missing publisher text without notice.

rubricAnchors.score1: An accuracy, confidence, source-authority, analyst-quote, or price-target claim appears unsupported, or the AI summary stands in for missing publisher text without notice.
rubricAnchors.score3: Most claims trace to a visible source but one AI-summary point reads as established fact or loses its source link.
rubricAnchors.score5: Every claim traces to a visible source passage, no-source-for-point is shown honestly when no source backs a point, and no accuracy, authority-ranking, analyst, or price-target claim appears anywhere.

adjudicationNotes: This is a zero-tolerance case for layer blurring: any screen that merges sourced publisher text, AI summary, context, or other publishers into a single Newswire-authored narrative is an automatic fail on evidence-discipline and product-appropriateness, regardless of polish.
Sentence-level source trace is load-bearing: an output that attaches an AI summary without per-point source links fails product-appropriateness and implementation-clarity.
The no-source-for-point state tests honest synthesis: an output that silently fills gaps with confident-sounding summary sentences fails evidence-discipline.
Context is machine-assembled background, never editorial analysis: an output that lets the context panel read as a price forecast or buy/sell recommendation fails product-appropriateness.
Other publishers must be neutral: an output that ranks publishers by reliability or marks a 'best source' fails evidence-discipline.
Do not prescribe exact copy, brand colours, or pixel-level layout; the brief fixes the layer-separation and source-trace constraints, not the visual identity.
